confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_20c87d21-c373-4451-b284-68b3aa4c8cd8 read back as received

payload.body:
# Model CLI and API Agents as Execution Embodiments

## Summary

Operator and agent observed that CLI-based agents and API-based agents are not different species of intelligence. A CLI agent is an API-like agent given an operator-inhabitable embodiment: terminal, working directory, shell, logs, interrupts, prompts, and visible process locality.

The terminal is a portal into the same underlying execution world, but with inspectability, interruptibility, locality, and shared operational presence.

## Evidence

- CLI sessions expose commands, logs, failures, prompts, long-running state, working directory, PATH, credentials, OS session, window title, desktop, and operator interrupts.
- API-only sessions can perform similar intelligence and tool calls, but the operator cannot inhabit the unfolding process through the same terminal surface.
- This distinction matters for Narada operator-surface doctrine, session binding, resume semantics, and authority routing across mixed CLI/API coding agents.

## Proposal

- Document CLI agents and API agents as different embodiments of agentic execution, not different classes of intelligence.
- Define terminal windows as operator-inhabitable portals over API-equivalent execution worlds, with explicit affordances: locality, inspectability, interruptibility, traceability, and shared operational presence.
- Use this framing when designing `narada resume`, session binding, operator surface adapters, and authority assignment between API-based and CLI-based agents.

## Recommendation

Admit as doctrine/product design input for operator surface topology and agent embodiment documentation.

front_matter.title: Model CLI and API agents as execution embodiments
